testCases.title: Successful user registration with valid Email and Password
preconditions: User is on the mobile Registration screen.
Device has an active internet connection.
steps: Enter a unique, valid email address (e.g., 'newuser@example.com').
Enter a password meeting complexity requirements (e.g., 'Password123!').
testCases.expectedResult: User account is successfully created. User is redirected to the dashboard or a 'Welcome' screen. A confirmation message is displayed if applicable.

testCases.title: Attempt to register with an already existing email address
preconditions: User is on the mobile Registration screen.
An account with 'existinguser@example.com' already exists.
Device has an active internet connection.
steps: Enter an email address that is already registered (e.g., 'existinguser@example.com').
testCases.expectedResult: An error message indicating 'Email already registered' or 'Account with this email already exists' is displayed. User remains on the Registration screen.

testCases.title: Attempt to register with empty required fields
preconditions: User is on the mobile Registration screen.
Device has an active internet connection.
testCases.expectedResult: Error messages are displayed for each empty required field (e.g., 'Email is required', 'Password is required'). Button remains disabled or user remains on the Registration screen.

testCases.title: Network interruption during user registration
preconditions: User is on the mobile Registration screen.
Device has an active internet connection.
steps: Immediately disable network connection (e.g., airplane mode) before the request completes.
testCases.expectedResult: An appropriate network error message is displayed (e.g., 'No internet connection', 'Could not connect to server'). User remains on the Registration screen.

testCases.title: Successful login with valid Email and Password
preconditions: User is on the mobile Login screen.
An account 'user@example.com' with password 'Password123!' is registered.
Device has an active internet connection.
testCases.expectedResult: User is successfully logged in and redirected to the dashboard. Login time is under 3 seconds.

testCases.title: Attempt to login with incorrect password
preconditions: User is on the mobile Login screen.
An account 'user@example.com' is registered.
Device has an active internet connection.
testCases.expectedResult: An error message indicating 'Incorrect email or password' is displayed. User remains on the Login screen.

testCases.title: Attempt to login with unregistered email
preconditions: User is on the mobile Login screen.
Email 'unregistered@example.com' does not exist in the system.
Device has an active internet connection.
testCases.expectedResult: An error message indicating 'Incorrect email or password' is displayed. User remains on the Login screen.

testCases.title: Account locked after multiple failed login attempts
preconditions: User is on the mobile Login screen.
An account 'user@example.com' is registered.
System has a defined threshold for failed login attempts leading to account lock (e.g., 5 attempts).
Device has an active internet connection.
steps: Repeatedly attempt to log in with correct email 'user@example.com' but incorrect password until account lock threshold is reached.
testCases.expectedResult: After exceeding the threshold, an error message indicating 'Account locked. Please use Forgot Password or contact support' is displayed. Subsequent login attempts with correct credentials for the locked account fail.

testCases.title: Successful login/registration for a new user via Google SSO
preconditions: User is on the mobile Login/Registration screen.
User has a Google account on their device that is not yet registered with the app.
Device has an active internet connection.
steps: Tap the 'Sign in with Google' button.
Select a Google account to proceed (if multiple are available).
testCases.expectedResult: User account is successfully created/linked via Google. User is logged in and redirected to the dashboard.

testCases.title: Successful login for an existing user via Google SSO (account already linked)
preconditions: User is on the mobile Login screen.
An existing user account is already linked to a Google account.
Device has an active internet connection.
steps: Tap the 'Sign in with Google' button.
Select the linked Google account (if multiple are available).
testCases.expectedResult: User is successfully logged in via Google SSO and redirected to the dashboard.

testCases.title: User cancels Google authentication prompt
preconditions: User is on the mobile Login screen.
Device has an active internet connection.
steps: Tap the 'Sign in with Google' button.
On the Google authentication prompt (e.g., account selection or permission request), tap 'Cancel' or navigate back.
testCases.expectedResult: User is returned to the app's Login screen. No account creation or login occurs. No error message is displayed (as it's a user-initiated cancellation).

testCases.title: User revokes app permissions from Google account directly
preconditions: User has previously logged in via Google SSO and granted permissions.
User has revoked the app's access from their Google account settings (outside the app).
User is on the mobile Login screen.
Device has an active internet connection.
steps: Tap the 'Sign in with Google' button.
Select the previously linked Google account.
testCases.expectedResult: The Google authentication flow should prompt the user to re-grant permissions or inform them that access has been revoked. Upon re-granting, the user should be logged in.

testCases.title: Successful Forgot Password flow for a registered email
preconditions: User is on the mobile Login screen.
An account 'user@example.com' is registered.
Device has an active internet connection.
User has access to the 'user@example.com' inbox.
steps: Tap 'Forgot Password?' link/button on the Login screen.
Enter the registered email address (e.g., 'user@example.com') on the 'Forgot Password' screen.
Check the email inbox for the password reset link/code.
Tap the link or enter the code in the app.
Enter a new password meeting complexity requirements.
testCases.expectedResult: A success message (e.g., 'Password reset successfully') is displayed. User is redirected to the Login screen. The user can successfully log in with the new password.

testCases.title: Attempt Forgot Password for an unregistered email
preconditions: User is on the mobile Login screen.
Email 'unregistered@example.com' does not exist in the system.
Device has an active internet connection.
testCases.expectedResult: A message indicating 'If an account with this email exists, a password reset link has been sent' or similar (to prevent email enumeration) is displayed. No email is sent to the unregistered address. User remains on the 'Forgot Password' screen or is redirected to login.

testCases.title: Use of an expired or invalid password reset link/code
preconditions: User has initiated a 'Forgot Password' request for 'user@example.com' and received a link/code.
The reset link/code has expired (e.g., after 15 minutes) or been invalidated (e.g., by requesting a new one).
Device has an active internet connection.
steps: Attempt to use the expired/invalid password reset link or enter the expired/invalid code.
testCases.expectedResult: An error message indicating 'Password reset link/code is expired or invalid. Please request a new one.' is displayed. User is prompted to request a new reset.

testCases.title: Request multiple password resets in quick succession
preconditions: User is on the mobile 'Forgot Password' screen.
An account 'user@example.com' is registered.
Device has an active internet connection.
steps: Immediately repeat the process several times (e.g., 3-5 times) for the same email.
testCases.expectedResult: The system should implement rate limiting. Only the most recent password reset link/code should be valid. Subsequent requests might result in a message like 'Please wait before requesting another reset' or render previous links/codes invalid.

testCases.title: Email validation: Invalid format (missing '@')
preconditions: User is on the Registration or Forgot Password screen.
steps: Attempt to proceed (e.g., tap 'Register' or 'Reset Password').
testCases.expectedResult: An error message 'Invalid email format' or similar is displayed below the Email field. User cannot proceed.

testCases.title: Email validation: Invalid format (missing domain)
preconditions: User is on the Registration or Forgot Password screen.
testCases.expectedResult: An error message 'Invalid email format' or similar is displayed. User cannot proceed.

testCases.title: Password complexity: Password too short
preconditions: User is on the Registration or New Password screen (Forgot Password flow).
steps: In the Password field, enter a password shorter than the minimum requirement (e.g., 'Pass1').
Attempt to proceed (e.g., tap 'Register' or 'Set New Password').
testCases.expectedResult: An error message 'Password must be at least X characters long' is displayed. User cannot proceed.

testCases.title: Password complexity: Password missing required character types
preconditions: User is on the Registration or New Password screen (Forgot Password flow).
steps: In the Password field, enter a password that meets length but misses a required character type (e.g., 'password123' if uppercase is required, or 'PASSWORD123' if lowercase is required, or 'Password!!!' if a number is required).
testCases.expectedResult: An error message detailing the missing complexity requirement (e.g., 'Password must contain at least one uppercase letter', 'Password must contain at least one number') is displayed. User cannot proceed.

testCases.title: Password fields do not match during registration/reset
preconditions: User is on the Registration or New Password screen.
steps: Enter a valid password in the 'Password' field (e.g., 'Password123!').
Enter a different password in the 'Confirm Password' field (e.g., 'Password123@').
testCases.expectedResult: An error message 'Passwords do not match' is displayed. User cannot proceed.

testCases.title: Verify system supports 2FA (underlying capability)
preconditions: User account has 2FA enabled at the backend level (manual setup for testing).
User logs in from a new device/browser.
steps: Perform a standard login with a 2FA-enabled account (e.g., '2fauser@example.com').
testCases.expectedResult: User is prompted for a second factor (e.g., OTP via authenticator app, SMS code) after successful email/password entry. Login completes successfully after valid 2FA input. This confirms the system's underlying ability to enforce 2FA.

testCases.title: Measure average login time
preconditions: User is on the mobile Login screen.
An account 'user@example.com' with password 'Password123!' is registered.
Device has a stable internet connection.
steps: Enter registered email address and correct password.
Stop the timer when the dashboard or home screen fully loads.
testCases.expectedResult: The measured login time is consistently under 3 seconds across multiple attempts.
